1.1: In my first book, O Theophilus (friend of god), I wrote about all that Jesus (YHWH saves) began to do and to teach {didaskein},
1.2: until the day He was taken up to heaven, after giving instructions through the Holy Spirit {Hagiou Pneumatos} to the apostles (sent ones) He had chosen.
1.3: After His suffering, He presented Himself to them with many convincing proofs that He was alive. He appeared to them over a span of forty days and spoke about the kingdom of God {Theou}.
1.4: And while they were gathered together (eating together), He commanded them: “Do not leave Jerusalem (city of peace), but wait for the gift the Father promised, which you? have heard Me discuss.
1.5: For John (YHWH is gracious) baptized (submerged) with water, but in a few days you? will be baptized with the Holy Spirit {Hagi? Pneumati}.
1.6: So when they came together, they asked Him, “Lord {Kyrie}, will You at this time restore the kingdom to Israel (He wrestles with God)?
1.7: Jesus replied, “It is not for you? to know times or seasons that the Father {Pat?r} has fixed by His own authority.
1.8: But you? will receive power {dynamin} when the Holy Spirit {Hagiou Pneumatos} comes upon you?, and you? will be My witnesses in Jerusalem (city of peace), and in all Judea (land of praise) and Samaria, and to the ends of the earth.
1.9: After He had said this, they watched as He was taken up, and a cloud hid Him from their sight.
1.10: They were looking intently into the sky as He was going, when suddenly (Behold) two men dressed in white stood beside them.
1.11: “Men of Galilee, they said, “why do you? stand here looking into the sky? This same Jesus (YHWH saves), who has been taken from you? into heaven, will come back in the same way you? have seen Him go into heaven.
1.12: Then they returned to Jerusalem (city of peace) from the Mount of Olives, which is near the city, a Sabbath day’s journey away <3,000 ft / 914.4 m>.
1.13: When they arrived, they went to the upper room where they were staying: Peter (rock) and John (YHWH is gracious), James (supplanter / follower) and Andrew (manly), Philip (friend of horses) and Thomas (twin), Bartholomew (son of Talmai) and Matthew (Gift of YHWH), James son of Alphaeus (exchange), Simon (hearing) the Zealot, and Judas son of James.
1.14: With one accord they all continued in prayer, along with the women and Mary (beloved) the mother of Jesus (YHWH saves), and with His brothers.
1.15: In those days Peter (rock) stood up among the brothers (a gathering of about a hundred and twenty) and said,
1.16: “Brothers, the Scripture had to be fulfilled that the Holy Spirit {Hagion Pneuma} foretold through the mouth of David (beloved) concerning Judas (praise), who became a guide for those who arrested Jesus (YHWH saves).
1.17: He was one of our number and shared in this ministry {diakonias}.
1.18: (Now with the reward for his wickedness (unrighteousness) Judas bought a field; there he fell headlong and burst open in the middle, and all his intestines spilled out.
1.19: This became known to all who lived in Jerusalem (city of peace), so they called that field in their own language Akeldama, that is, Field of Blood.)
1.20: “For it is written in the book (papyrus roll) of Psalms: ‘May his place be deserted; let there be no one to dwell in it ,’ and, ‘May another take his position .’
1.21: Therefore it is necessary to choose one of the men who have accompanied us the whole time the Lord {Kyrios} Jesus (YHWH saves) went in and out among us,
1.22: beginning from John’s (YHWH is gracious) baptism (submersion) until the day Jesus was taken up from us. For one of these must become a witness with us of His resurrection (rising again).
1.23: So they proposed two men: Joseph (he will add) called Barsabbas (also known as Justus (righteous)) and Matthias.
1.24: And they prayed, “Lord {Kyrie}, You know everyone’s heart. Show us which of these two You have chosen
1.25: to take up this ministry {diakonias} and apostleship, which Judas (praise) abandoned to go to his rightful place.
1.26: Then they cast lots, and the lot (covering) fell to Matthias. So he was added to the eleven apostles (sent ones).

2.1: When the day of Pentecost (Shavuot) came, they were all together in one place.
2.2: Suddenly a sound like a mighty rushing wind came from heaven and filled the whole house where they were sitting.
2.3: They saw tongues (languages) like flames of fire that separated and came to rest on each of them.
2.4: And they were all filled with the Holy Spirit {Hagiou Pneumatos} and began to speak in other tongues (languages) as the Spirit {Pneuma} enabled them.
2.5: Now there were dwelling (staying) in Jerusalem (city of peace) God-fearing Jews (people of praise) from every nation {ethnous} under heaven.
2.6: And when this sound rang out, a crowd came together in bewilderment, because each one heard them speaking his own language.
2.7: Astounded and amazed, they asked, “Are not all these who are speaking Galileans?
2.8: How is it then that each of us hears them in his own native language?
2.9: Parthians, Medes, and Elamites; residents of Mesopotamia, Judea (land of praise) and Cappadocia, Pontus and Asia (the Roman Province of Asia),
2.10: Phrygia and Pamphylia, Egypt (land of bondage) and the parts of Libya near Cyrene; visitors from Rome,
2.11: both Jews (people of praise) and converts to Judaism; Cretans and Arabs—we hear them declaring the wonders of God {Theou} in our own tongues (languages)!
2.12: Astounded and perplexed, they asked one another, “What does this mean?
2.13: But others mocked them and said, “They are drunk on new wine!
2.14: Then Peter (rock) stood up with the Eleven, lifted up his voice, and addressed the crowd: “Men of Judea and all who dwell in Jerusalem (city of peace), let this be known to you?, and listen carefully to my words {rh?mata}.
2.15: These men are not drunk, as you? suppose. It is only the third hour of the day (9:00 AM)!
2.16: No, this is what was spoken by the prophet Joel (YHWH is God):
2.17: ‘In the last days, God {Theos} says, I will pour out My Spirit {Pneumatos} on all people. Your? sons and daughters will prophesy, your? young men will see visions, your? old men will dream dreams.
2.18: Even on My menservants {doulous} and maidservants {doulas} I will pour out My Spirit {Pneumatos} in those days, and they will prophesy.
2.19: I will show wonders in the heavens above and signs on the earth below, blood and fire and billows of smoke.
2.20: The sun will be turned to darkness, and the moon to blood, before the coming of the great and glorious Day of the Lord {Kyriou}.
2.21: And everyone who calls on the name of the Lord {Kyriou} will be saved .’
2.22: Men of Israel (He wrestles with God), listen to this message: Jesus (YHWH saves) of Nazareth (branch) was a man certified by God {Theou} to you? by miracles, wonders, and signs, which God {Theos} did among you? through Him, as you? yourselves know.
2.23: He was delivered up by God’s {Theou} set plan and foreknowledge, and you?, by the hands of the lawless, put Him to death by nailing Him to the cross.
2.24: But God {Theos} raised Him from the dead, releasing Him from the agony of death, because it was impossible for death to keep Him in its grip.
2.25: David (beloved) says about Him: ‘I saw \I foresaw/ the Lord {Kyrion} always before me; because He is at my right hand, I will not be shaken.
2.26: Therefore my heart is glad and my tongue (language) rejoices; my body (flesh) also will dwell in hope,
2.27: because You will not abandon my soul {psych?n} to Hades, nor will You let Your Holy One see decay.
2.28: You have made known to me the paths of life; You will fill me with joy in Your presence .’
2.29: Brothers, I can tell you? with confidence that the patriarch David (beloved) died and was buried, and his tomb is with us to this day.
2.30: But he was a prophet and knew that God {Theos} had promised him on oath that He would place one of his descendants on his throne .
2.31: Foreseeing this, David spoke about the resurrection (rising again) of the Christ (the Anointed One), that He was not abandoned to Hades, nor did His body (flesh) see decay.
2.32: God {Theos} has raised this Jesus to life, to which we are all witnesses.
2.33: Exalted, then, to the right hand of God {Theou}, He has received from the Father the promised Holy Spirit {Hagiou Pneumatos} and has poured out what you? now see and hear.
2.34: For David (beloved) did not ascend into heaven, but he himself says: ‘The Lord {Kyrios} said to my Lord {Kyri?}, “Sit at My right hand
2.35: until I make Your enemies a footstool for Your feet .”’
2.36: Therefore let all Israel (He wrestles with God) know with certainty that God {Theos} has made this Jesus (YHWH saves), whom you? crucified, both Lord {Kyrion} and Christ (the Anointed One)!
2.37: When the people heard this, they were cut to the heart and asked Peter (rock) and the other apostles (sent ones), “Brothers, what shall we do?
2.38: Peter (rock) replied, “Repent and be baptized (submerged), every one of you?, in the name of Jesus (YHWH saves) Christ (the Anointed One) for the forgiveness of your? sins, and you? will receive the gift of the Holy Spirit {Hagiou Pneumatos}.
2.39: This promise belongs to you? and your? children and to all who are far off—to all whom the Lord {Kyrios} our God {Theos} will call to Himself.
2.40: With many other words he testified, and he urged them, “Be saved from this corrupt generation.
2.41: Those who embraced his message were baptized (submerged), and about three thousand were added to the believers that day \about three thousand souls were added that day/.
2.42: They devoted themselves to the apostles’ (sent ones) teaching {didach?} and to the fellowship {koin?nia}, to the breaking of bread and to prayer.
2.43: A sense of awe (fear) came over everyone, and the apostles (sent ones) performed many wonders and signs.
2.44: All the believers were together and had everything in common.
2.45: Selling their possessions and goods, they shared with anyone who was in need.
2.46: With one accord they continued to meet daily in the temple courts \the temple/ and to break bread from house to house, sharing their meals with gladness and sincerity of heart,
2.47: praising God {Theon} and enjoying the favor of all the people. And the Lord {Kyrios} added to their number daily those who were being saved.

3.1: One afternoon Peter (rock) and John (YHWH is gracious) were going up to the temple at the hour of prayer, the ninth hour (3:00 PM).
3.2: And a man who was lame from birth was being carried to the temple gate called Beautiful, where he was put every day to beg from those entering the temple courts \the temple/.
3.3: When he saw Peter (rock) and John (YHWH is gracious) about to enter, he asked them for money.
3.4: Peter (rock) looked directly at him, as did John (YHWH is gracious). “Look at us! said Peter.
3.5: So the man gave them his attention, expecting to receive something from them.
3.6: But Peter (rock) said, “Silver or gold I do not have, but what I have I give you: In the name of Jesus (YHWH saves) Christ (the Anointed One) of Nazareth (branch), get up and walk [In the name of Jesus Christ of Nazareth, walk!]!
3.7: Taking him by the right hand, Peter helped him up, and at once the man’s feet and ankles were made strong.
3.8: He sprang to his feet and began to walk. Then he went with them into the temple courts, walking and leaping and praising God {Theon}.
3.9: When all the people saw him walking and praising God {Theon},
3.10: they recognized him as the man who used to sit begging at the Beautiful Gate of the temple, and they were filled with wonder and amazement at what had happened to him.
3.11: While the man clung to Peter (rock) and John (YHWH is gracious), all the people were astonished and ran to them in the walkway called Solomon’s (peace) Colonnade.
3.12: And when Peter (rock) saw this, he addressed the people: “Men of Israel (He wrestles with God), why are you? surprised by this? Why do you? stare at us as if by our own power {dynamei} or godliness we had made this man walk?
3.13: The God {Theos} of Abraham (father of many), Isaac (he will laugh), and Jacob (he grasps the heel), the God of our fathers {pater?n}, has glorified His servant (His child) Jesus (YHWH saves). You? handed Him over and rejected Him before Pilate (armed with a spear), even though he had decided to release Him.
3.14: You? rejected the Holy and Righteous One and asked that a murderer be released to you?.
3.15: You? killed the Author (pioneer) of life, but God {Theos} raised Him from the dead, and we are witnesses of this fact.
3.16: By faith in the name of Jesus (YHWH saves), this man whom you? see and know has been made strong. It is Jesus’ name and the faith that comes through Him that has given him this complete healing in your? presence.
3.17: And now, brothers, I know that you? acted in ignorance, as did your? leaders.
3.18: But in this way God has fulfilled what He foretold through all the prophets, saying that His Christ (the Anointed One) would suffer.
3.19: Repent, then, and turn back, so that your? sins may be wiped away,
3.20: that times of refreshing may come from the presence of the Lord {Kyriou}, and that He may send Jesus (YHWH saves), the Christ (the Anointed One), who has been appointed for you?.
3.21: Heaven must take Him in until the time comes for the restoration of all things, which God {Theos} announced long ago through His holy prophets.
3.22: For Moses (drawn out) said, ‘The Lord {Kyrios} your? God {Theos} will raise up for you? a prophet like me from among your? brothers. You? must listen to Him in everything He tells you? .
3.23: Everyone who does not listen to Him will be completely cut off from among his people .
3.24: Indeed, all the prophets from Samuel on, as many as have spoken, have proclaimed these days.
3.25: And you? are sons of the prophets and of the covenant {diath?k?s} God {Theos} made with your? fathers {pateras} when He said to Abraham (father of many), ‘Through your offspring (seed) all the families of the earth will be blessed .’
3.26: When God {Theos} raised up His Servant, He sent Him first to you? to bless you? by turning each of you from your? wicked ways.

4.1: While Peter and John were speaking to the people, the priests and the captain of the temple guard and the Sadducees came up to them,
4.2: greatly disturbed that they were teaching {didaskein} the people and proclaiming in Jesus (YHWH saves) the resurrection (rising again) of the dead.
4.3: They seized Peter and John, and because it was evening, they put them in custody until the next day.
4.4: But many who heard the message believed (had faith), and the number of men grew to about five thousand.
4.5: The next day the rulers, elders, and scribes assembled in Jerusalem (city of peace),
4.6: along with Annas the high priest, Caiaphas, John (YHWH is gracious), Alexander (defending men), and many others from the high priest’s family.
4.7: They had Peter and John brought in and began to question them: “By what power {dynamei} or what name did you? do this?
4.8: Then Peter (rock), filled with the Holy Spirit {Hagiou Pneumatos}, said to them, “Rulers and elders of the people!
4.9: If we are being examined today about a kind service to a man who was lame, to determine how he was healed,
4.10: then let this be known to all of you? and to all the people of Israel (He wrestles with God): It is by the name of Jesus (YHWH saves) Christ (the Anointed One) of Nazareth (branch), whom you? crucified but whom God {Theos} raised from the dead, that this man stands before you? healed.
4.11: This Jesus is ‘the stone you? builders rejected, which has become the cornerstone .’
4.12: Salvation exists in no one else, for there is no other name under heaven given to men by which we must be saved.
4.13: When they saw the boldness of Peter (rock) and John (YHWH is gracious) and realized that they were unschooled, ordinary men, they marveled and took note that these men had been with Jesus (YHWH saves).
4.14: And seeing the man who had been healed standing there with them, they had nothing to say in response.
4.15: So they ordered them to leave the Sanhedrin (the Council) and then conferred together.
4.16: “What shall we do with these men? they asked. “It is clear to everyone living in Jerusalem (city of peace) that a remarkable miracle has occurred through them, and we cannot deny it.
4.17: But to keep this message from spreading any further among the people, we must warn them not to speak to anyone in this name.
4.18: Then they called them in again and commanded them not to speak or teach {didaskein} at all in the name of Jesus (YHWH saves).
4.19: But Peter (rock) and John (YHWH is gracious) replied, “Judge for yourselves whether it is right in God’s sight to listen to you? rather than God {Theou}.
4.20: For we cannot stop speaking about what we have seen and heard.
4.21: After further threats they let them go. They could not find a way to punish them, because all the people were glorifying God {Theon} for what had happened.
4.22: For the man who was miraculously healed was over forty years old.
4.23: On their release, Peter and John returned to their own people and reported everything that the chief priests and elders had said to them.
4.24: When the believers heard this, they lifted up their voices to God {Theon} with one accord. “Sovereign Lord {Despota}, they said, “You made the heaven and the earth and the sea and everything in them.
4.25: You spoke by the Holy Spirit {Hagiou Pneumatos} through the mouth of Your servant (child), our father David (beloved): ‘Why do the nations {ethn?} rage and the peoples plot in vain?
4.26: The kings of the earth take their stand and the rulers gather together against the Lord {Kyriou} and against His Anointed One (His Christ).’
4.27: In fact, this is the very city where Herod (heroic) and Pontius Pilate (armed with a spear) conspired with the Gentiles {ethnesin} and the people of Israel (He wrestles with God) against Your holy servant Jesus (YHWH saves), whom You anointed.
4.28: They carried out what Your hand and will had decided beforehand would happen.
4.29: And now, Lord {Kyrie}, consider their threats, and enable Your servants {doulois} to speak Your word with complete boldness,
4.30: as You stretch out Your hand to heal and perform signs and wonders through the name of Your holy servant Jesus (YHWH saves).
4.31: After they had prayed, their meeting place was shaken, and they were all filled with the Holy Spirit {Hagiou Pneumatos} and spoke the word of God {Theou} boldly.
4.32: The multitude of believers was one in heart and soul {psych?}. No one claimed that any of his possessions was his own, but they shared everything they owned.
4.33: With great power {dynamei} the apostles (sent ones) continued to give their testimony about the resurrection (rising again) of the Lord {Kyriou} Jesus (YHWH saves). And abundant grace was upon them all.
4.34: There were no needy ones among them, because those who owned lands or houses would sell their property, bring the proceeds from the sales,
4.35: and lay them at the apostles’ (sent ones) feet for distribution to anyone as he had need.
4.36: Joseph (he will add), a Levite from Cyprus, whom the apostles (sent ones) called Barnabas (son of encouragement) (meaning Son of Encouragement),
4.37: sold a field he owned, brought the money, and laid it at the apostles’ (sent ones) feet.

5.1: Now a man named Ananias (YHWH is gracious), together with his wife Sapphira (sapphire), also sold a piece of property.
5.2: With his wife’s full knowledge, he kept back some of the proceeds for himself, but brought a portion and laid it at the apostles’ (sent ones) feet.
5.3: Then Peter (rock) said, “Ananias (YHWH is gracious), how is it that Satan (the adversary) has filled your heart to lie to the Holy Spirit {Hagion Pneuma} and withhold some of the proceeds from the land?
5.4: Did it not belong to you before it was sold? And after it was sold, was it not at your disposal? How could you conceive such a deed in your heart? You have not lied to men, but to God {The?}!
5.5: On hearing these words, Ananias (YHWH is gracious) fell down and died. And great fear came over all who heard what had happened.
5.6: Then the young men stepped forward, wrapped up his body, and carried him out and buried him.
5.7: About three hours later his wife also came in, unaware of what had happened.
5.8: “Tell me, said Peter (rock), “is this the price you? and your? husband got for the land? “Yes, she answered, “that is the price.
5.9: “How could you? agree to test the Spirit {Pneuma} of the Lord {Kyriou}? Peter replied. “Look (Behold), the feet of the men who buried your husband are at the door, and they will carry you out also.
5.10: At that instant she fell down at his feet and died. Then the young men came in and, finding her dead, carried her out and buried her beside her husband.
5.11: And great fear came over the whole church {ekkl?sian} and all who heard about these events.
5.12: The apostles (sent ones) performed many signs and wonders among the people, and with one accord the believers gathered together in Solomon’s (peace) Colonnade.
5.13: Although the people regarded them highly, no one else dared to join them.
5.14: Yet more and more believers were brought to the Lord {Kyri?}—large numbers of both men and women.
5.15: As a result, people brought the sick into the streets and laid them on cots and mats, so that at least Peter’s shadow might fall on some of them as he passed by.
5.16: Crowds also gathered from the towns around Jerusalem (city of peace), bringing the sick and those tormented by unclean (impure) spirits {pneumat?n}, and all of them were healed.
5.17: Then the high priest and all his associates, who belonged to the party of the Sadducees, were filled with jealousy. They went out
5.18: and arrested the apostles (sent ones) and put them in the public jail.
5.19: But during the night an angel (messenger) of the Lord {Kyriou} opened the doors of the jail and brought them out, saying,
5.20: “Go, stand in the temple courts \the temple/ and tell the people the full message {rh?mata} of this new life.
5.21: At daybreak the apostles entered the temple courts as they had been told and began to teach the people. When the high priest and his associates arrived, they convened the Sanhedrin (the Council) the full assembly of the elders of Israel (He wrestles with God)—and sent to the jail for the apostles.
5.22: But on arriving at the jail, the officers did not find them there. So they returned with the report:
5.23: “We found the jail securely locked, with the guards posted at the doors; but when we opened them, we found no one inside.
5.24: When the captain of the temple guard and the chief priests heard this account, they were perplexed as to what was happening \as to what this might be/.
5.25: Then someone came in and announced, “Look (Behold), the men you? put in jail are standing in the temple courts teaching {didaskontes} the people!
5.26: At that point, the captain went with the officers and brought the apostles—but not by force, for fear the people would stone them.
5.27: They brought them in and made them stand before the Sanhedrin (the Council), where the high priest interrogated them.
5.28: “We gave you? strict orders not to teach {didaskein} in this name, he said. “Yet you? have filled Jerusalem (city of peace) with your? teaching {didach?s} and are determined to make us responsible for this man’s blood.
5.29: But Peter (rock) and the other apostles (sent ones) replied, “We must obey God {The?} rather than men.
5.30: The God {Theos} of our fathers {pater?n} raised up Jesus (YHWH saves), whom you? had killed by hanging Him on a tree.
5.31: God {Theos} exalted Him to His right hand as Prince and Savior {S?t?ra}, in order to grant repentance and forgiveness of sins to Israel (He wrestles with God).
5.32: We are witnesses of these things {rh?mat?n}, and so is the Holy Spirit {Hagion Pneuma}, whom God {Theos} has given to those who obey Him.
5.33: When the Council members heard this, they were enraged, and they resolved [they took counsel] to put the apostles to death.
5.34: But a Pharisee named Gamaliel (my reward is God), a teacher of the law who was honored by all the people, stood up in the Sanhedrin (the Council) and ordered that the men be put outside for a short time.
5.35: “Men of Israel (He wrestles with God), he said, “consider carefully what you? are about to do to these men.
5.36: Some time ago Theudas rose up, claiming to be somebody, and about four hundred men joined him. He was killed, all his followers were dispersed, and it all came to nothing.
5.37: After him, Judas (praise) the Galilean appeared in the days of the census and drew away people after him. He too perished, and all his followers were scattered.
5.38: So in the present case I advise you?: Leave these men alone. Let them go! For if their purpose or endeavor is of human origin, it will fail.
5.39: But if it is from God {Theou}, you? will not be able to stop them. You? may even find yourselves fighting against God {theomachoi}.
5.40: At this, they yielded to Gamaliel. They called the apostles in and had them flogged. Then they ordered them not to speak in the name of Jesus (YHWH saves), and released them.
5.41: The apostles left the Sanhedrin (the Council), rejoicing that they had been counted worthy of suffering disgrace for the Name {Onomatos}.
5.42: Every day, in the temple courts and from house to house, they did not stop teaching {didaskontes} and proclaiming the good news that Jesus (YHWH saves) is the Christ (the Anointed One).

6.1: In those days when the disciples (pupils) were increasing in number, the Grecian Jews among them began to grumble against the Hebraic Jews \the Hellenists began to grumble against the Hebrews/ because their widows were being overlooked in the daily distribution of food.
6.2: So the Twelve summoned all the disciples (pupils) and said, “It is unacceptable for us to neglect the word of God {Theou} in order to wait on tables.
6.3: Therefore, brothers, select from among you? seven men confirmed to be full of the Spirit {Pneumatos} and wisdom. We will assign this responsibility to them
6.4: and will devote ourselves to prayer and to the ministry {diakonia} of the word.
6.5: This proposal pleased the whole group. They chose Stephen (that which surrounds), a man full of faith and of the Holy Spirit {Hagiou Pneumatos}, as well as Philip (friend of horses), Prochorus (leader of the dance), Nicanor, Timon (to honor), Parmenas, and Nicolas from Antioch, a convert to Judaism \and Nicolas, a convert of Antioch/.
6.6: They presented these seven to the apostles \—whom they set before the apostles,/, who prayed and laid their hands on them.
6.7: So the word of God {Theou} continued to spread. The number of disciples (pupils) in Jerusalem (city of peace) grew rapidly, and a great number of priests became obedient to the faith.
6.8: Now Stephen (that which surrounds), who was full of grace and power {dyname?s}, was performing great wonders and signs among the people.
6.9: But resistance arose from what was called the Synagogue of the Freedmen, including Cyrenians, Alexandrians, and men from the provinces of Cilicia and Asia \and those from Cilicia and Asia/. They disputed with Stephen (that which surrounds),
6.10: but they could not stand up to his wisdom or the Spirit {Pneumati} by whom he spoke.
6.11: Then they prompted some men to say, “We heard Stephen speak words {rh?mata} of blasphemy against Moses (drawn out) and against God {Theon}.
6.12: So they stirred up the people, elders, and scribes and confronted Stephen. They seized him and brought him before the Sanhedrin (the Council),
6.13: where they presented false witnesses who said, “This man never stops speaking against this holy place and against the law.
6.14: For we have heard him say that Jesus (YHWH saves) of Nazareth (branch) will destroy this place and change the customs that Moses (drawn out) handed down to us.
6.15: All who were sitting in the Sanhedrin (the Council) looked intently at Stephen, and they saw that his face was like the face of an angel (messenger).

7.1: Then the high priest asked Stephen, “Are these charges true?
7.2: And Stephen declared: “Brothers and fathers {pateres}, listen to me! The God {Theos} of glory {dox?s} appeared to our father Abraham (father of many) while he was still in Mesopotamia, before he lived in Haran (hill),
7.3: and told him, ‘Leave your country and your kindred and go to the land I will show you .’
7.4: So Abraham left the land of the Chaldeans and settled in Haran (hill). After his father {patera} died, God brought him out of that place and into this land where you? are now living.
7.5: He gave him no inheritance here, not even a foot of ground. But God promised to give possession of the land to Abraham and his descendants (seed), even though he did not yet have a child.
7.6: God {Theos} told him that his descendants (seed) would be foreigners in a strange land, and that they would be enslaved and mistreated four hundred years.
7.7: ‘But I will punish the nation {ethnos} that enslaves them, God {Theos} said, ‘and afterward they will come forth and worship Me in this place .’
7.8: Then God gave Abraham the covenant {diath?k?n} of circumcision, and Abraham became the father of Isaac (he will laugh) and circumcised him on the eighth day. And Isaac became the father of Jacob (he grasps the heel), and Jacob of the twelve patriarchs.
7.9: Because the patriarchs were jealous of Joseph (he will add), they sold him as a slave into Egypt (land of bondage). But God {Theos} was with him
7.10: and rescued him from all his troubles. He granted Joseph favor and wisdom in the sight of Pharaoh king of Egypt (land of bondage), who appointed him ruler over Egypt and all his household.
7.11: Then famine and great suffering swept across Egypt (land of bondage) and Canaan (low), and our fathers {pateres} could not find food.
7.12: When Jacob (he grasps the heel) heard that there was grain in Egypt (land of bondage), he sent our fathers {pateras} on their first visit.
7.13: On their second visit, Joseph (he will add) revealed his identity to (Joseph was made known to) his brothers, and his family became known to Pharaoh.
7.14: Then Joseph (he will add) sent for his father {patera} Jacob (he grasps the heel) and all his relatives, seventy-five in all.
7.15: So Jacob (he grasps the heel) went down to Egypt (land of bondage), where he and our fathers {pateres} died.
7.16: Their bones were carried back \And they were carried back/ to Shechem (shoulder) and placed in the tomb that Abraham (father of many) had bought from the sons of Hamor at Shechem for a price he paid in silver.
7.17: As the time drew near for God {Theos} to fulfill His promise to Abraham (father of many), our people in Egypt (land of bondage) increased greatly in number.
7.18: Then another king, who knew nothing of Joseph (he will add), arose over Egypt (land of bondage).
7.19: He exploited our people and oppressed (afflicted) our fathers {pateras}, forcing them to abandon their infants so they would die.
7.20: At that time Moses (drawn out) was born, and he was beautiful in the sight of God (he was of great status in God’s eyes). For three months he was nurtured in his father’s house.
7.21: When he was set outside, Pharaoh’s daughter took him and brought him up as her own son.
7.22: So Moses (drawn out) was educated in all the wisdom of the Egyptians (double straits) and was powerful in speech and action.
7.23: When Moses was forty years old, he decided to visit his brothers, the children of Israel (He wrestles with God).
7.24: And when he saw one of them being mistreated, Moses went to his defense and avenged him by striking down the Egyptian (double straits) who was oppressing him.
7.25: He assumed his brothers would understand that God {Theos} was using him to deliver them, but they did not.
7.26: The next day he came upon two Israelites who were fighting, and he tried to reconcile them, saying, ‘Men, you? are brothers. Why are you? mistreating each other?
7.27: But the man who was abusing his neighbor pushed Moses aside and said, ‘Who made you ruler and judge over us?
7.28: Do you want to kill me as you killed the Egyptian (double straits) yesterday ?’
7.29: At this remark, Moses (drawn out) fled to the land of Midian, where he lived as a foreigner and had two sons.
7.30: After forty years had passed, an angel (messenger) appeared to Moses in the flames of a burning bush in the desert near Mount Sinai.
7.31: When Moses (drawn out) saw it, he marveled at the sight. As he approached to look more closely, the voice of the Lord {Kyriou} came to him:
7.32: ‘I am the God {Theos} of your fathers {pater?n}, the God of Abraham (father of many), Isaac (he will laugh), and Jacob .’ Moses (drawn out) trembled with fear and did not dare to look.
7.33: Then the Lord {Kyrios} said to him, ‘Take off your sandals, for the place where you are standing is holy (set apart) ground.
7.34: I have indeed seen the oppression of My people in Egypt (land of bondage). I have heard their groaning and have come down to deliver them. Now come, I will send you back to Egypt .’
7.35: This Moses (drawn out), whom they had rejected with the words, ‘Who made you ruler and judge ? is the one whom God {Theos} sent to be their ruler and redeemer through the angel (Angel) who appeared to him in the bush.
7.36: He led them out and performed wonders and signs in the land of Egypt (land of bondage), at the Red Sea, and for forty years in the wilderness (solitary place).
7.37: This is the same Moses (drawn out) who told the Israelites, ‘God {Theos} will raise up for you? a prophet like me from among your? brothers .’
7.38: He was in the assembly {ekkl?sia} in the wilderness (solitary place) with the angel (messenger) who spoke to him on Mount Sinai, and with our fathers {pater?n}. And he received living words to pass on to us [to you].
7.39: But our fathers {pateres} refused to obey him. Instead, they rejected him and in their hearts turned back to Egypt (land of bondage).
7.40: They said to Aaron (exalted), ‘Make us gods {theous} who will go before us! As for this Moses (drawn out) who led us out of the land of Egypt (land of bondage), we do not know what has happened to him .’
7.41: At that time they made a calf and offered a sacrifice to the idol, rejoicing in the works (acts) of their hands.
7.42: But God {Theos} turned away from them and gave them over to the worship of the host of heaven, as it is written in the book (papyrus roll) of the prophets: ‘Did you? bring Me sacrifices and offerings forty years in the wilderness (solitary place), O house of Israel (He wrestles with God)?
7.43: You? have taken along the tabernacle of Molech and the star of your? god {theou} Rephan, the idols you? made to worship. Therefore I will send you? into exile beyond Babylon .’
7.44: Our fathers had the tabernacle of the Testimony with them in the wilderness (solitary place). It was constructed exactly as God had directed Moses (drawn out), according to the pattern he had seen.
7.45: And our fathers {pateres} who received it brought it in with Joshua (YHWH saves) when they dispossessed the nations {ethn?n} God {Theos} drove out before them. It remained until the time of David (beloved),
7.46: who found favor in the sight of God {Theou} and asked to provide a dwelling place for the God {The?} of Jacob [a dwelling place for the house of Jacob.].
7.47: But it was Solomon (peace) who built the house for Him.
7.48: However, the Most High does not dwell in houses made by human hands. As the prophet says:
7.49: ‘Heaven is My throne and the earth is My footstool. What kind of house will you? build for Me, says the Lord {Kyrios}, or where will My place of repose be?
7.50: Has not My hand made all these things ?’
7.51: You? stiff-necked people with uncircumcised hearts and ears! You? always resist the Holy Spirit {Hagi? Pneumati}, just as your? fathers did.
7.52: Which of the prophets did your? fathers {pateres} fail to persecute? They even killed those who foretold the coming of the Righteous One. And now you? are His betrayers and murderers—
7.53: you who received the law ordained by angels (messengers), yet have not kept it.
7.54: On hearing this, the members of the Sanhedrin were enraged \On hearing these things, they were cut in their hearts/, and they gnashed their teeth at him.
7.55: But Stephen, full of the Holy Spirit {Hagiou Pneumatos}, looked intently into heaven and saw the glory {doxan} of God {Theou} and Jesus (YHWH saves) standing at the right hand of God.
7.56: “Look (Behold), he said, “I see heaven open and the Son of Man standing at the right hand of God {Theou}.
7.57: At this they covered their ears, cried out in a loud voice, and rushed together at him.
7.58: They dragged him out of the city and began to stone him. Meanwhile the witnesses laid their garments at the feet of a young man named Saul (asked for).
7.59: While they were stoning him, Stephen (that which surrounds) appealed, “Lord {Kyrie} Jesus (YHWH saves), receive my spirit {pneuma}.
7.60: Falling on his knees, he cried out in a loud voice, “Lord {Kyrie}, do not hold this sin against them. And when he had said this, he fell asleep.

8.1: And Saul (asked for) was there, giving approval to Stephen’s death. On that day a great persecution broke out against the church {ekkl?sian} in Jerusalem (city of peace), and all except the apostles (sent ones) were scattered throughout Judea (land of praise) and Samaria.
8.2: God-fearing men buried Stephen (that which surrounds) and mourned deeply over him.
8.3: But Saul (asked for) began to destroy the church {ekkl?sian}. Going from house to house, he dragged off men and women and put them in prison.
8.4: Those who had been scattered preached the word wherever they went.
8.5: Philip (friend of horses) went down to a city in Samaria and proclaimed the Christ (the Anointed One) to them.
8.6: The crowds all paid close attention to Philip’s message and to the signs they saw him perform.
8.7: With loud shrieks, unclean (impure) spirits {pneumata} came out of many who were possessed, and many of the paralyzed and lame were healed.
8.8: So there was great joy in that city.
8.9: Prior to that time, a man named Simon (hearing) had practiced sorcery in the city and astounded the people {ethnos} of Samaria. He claimed to be someone great,
8.10: and all the people, from the least to the greatest, heeded his words and said, “This man is the divine power {dynamis} called the Great Power.
8.11: They paid close attention to him because he had astounded them for a long time with his sorcery.
8.12: But when they believed (had faith) Philip (friend of horses) as he preached the gospel (good news) of the kingdom of God {Theou} and the name of Jesus (YHWH saves) Christ (the Anointed One), they were baptized (submerged), both men and women.
8.13: Even Simon (hearing) himself believed (had faith) and was baptized (submerged). He followed Philip closely and was astounded by the great signs and miracles {dynameis} he observed.
8.14: When the apostles (sent ones) in Jerusalem (city of peace) heard that Samaria had received the word of God {Theou}, they sent Peter (rock) and John (YHWH is gracious) to them.
8.15: On their arrival, they prayed for them to receive the Holy Spirit {Hagion Pneuma}.
8.16: For the Holy Spirit had not yet fallen upon any of them; they had simply been baptized into the name of the Lord {kyriou} Jesus (YHWH saves).
8.17: Then Peter and John laid their hands on them, and they received the Holy Spirit {Hagion Pneuma}.
8.18: When Simon (hearing) saw that the Spirit {Pneuma} was given through the laying on of the apostles’ (sent ones) hands, he offered them money.
8.19: “Give me this power as well, he said, “so that everyone on whom I lay my hands may receive the Holy Spirit {Hagion Pneuma}.
8.20: But Peter (rock) replied, “May your silver perish with you, because you thought you could buy the gift of God {Theou} with money!
8.21: You have no part or share in our ministry, because your heart is not right before God {Theou}.
8.22: Repent, therefore, of your wickedness, and pray to the Lord {Kyriou}. Perhaps He will forgive you for the intent of your heart.
8.23: For I see that you are poisoned by bitterness and captive to iniquity.
8.24: Then Simon (hearing) answered, “Pray to the Lord {Kyrion} for me, so that nothing you? have said may happen to me.
8.25: And after Peter and John had testified and spoken the word of the Lord {Kyriou}, they returned to Jerusalem (city of peace), preaching the gospel in many of the Samaritan villages.
8.26: Now an angel (messenger) of the Lord {Kyriou} said to Philip (friend of horses), “Get up and go south to the desert road that goes down from Jerusalem (city of peace) to Gaza (strong).
8.27: So he started out, and on his way he met an Ethiopian eunuch, a court official in charge of the entire treasury of Candace {Kandak?}, queen of the Ethiopians. He had gone to Jerusalem (city of peace) to worship,
8.28: and on his return was sitting in his chariot reading Isaiah (YHWH saves) the prophet.
8.29: The Spirit {Pneuma} said to Philip (friend of horses), “Go over to that chariot and stay by it.
8.30: So Philip (friend of horses) ran up and heard the man reading Isaiah (YHWH saves) the prophet. “Do you understand what you are reading? Philip asked.
8.31: “How can I, he said, “unless someone guides me? And he invited Philip (friend of horses) to come up and sit with him.
8.32: The eunuch was reading this passage of Scripture: “He was led like a sheep to the slaughter, and as a lamb before the shearer is silent, so He did not open His mouth.
8.33: In His humiliation He was deprived of justice. Who can recount His descendants? For His life was removed from the earth .”
8.34: “Tell me, said the eunuch, “who is the prophet talking about, himself or someone else?
8.35: Then Philip (friend of horses) began with this very Scripture and told him the good news about Jesus (YHWH saves).
8.36: As they traveled along the road and came to some water, the eunuch said, “Look (Behold), here is water! What is there to prevent me from being baptized?” [37 And Philip said, “If you believe with all your heart, you may be baptized.” The eunuch replied, “I believe that Jesus Christ is the Son of God.”]
8.38: And he gave orders to stop the chariot. Then both Philip (friend of horses) and the eunuch went down into the water, and Philip baptized (submerged) him.
8.39: When they came up out of the water, the Spirit {Pneuma} of the Lord {Kyriou} carried Philip away, and the eunuch saw him no more, but went on his way rejoicing.
8.40: But Philip (friend of horses) appeared at Azotus and traveled through that region, preaching the gospel in all the towns until he came to Caesarea.

9.1: Meanwhile, Saul (asked for) was still breathing out murderous threats against the disciples (pupils) of the Lord {Kyriou}. He approached the high priest
9.2: and requested letters {epistolas} to the synagogues in Damascus (well-watered land), so that if he found any men or women belonging to the Way, he could bring them as prisoners to Jerusalem (city of peace).
9.3: As Saul drew near to Damascus (well-watered land) on his journey, suddenly a light from heaven flashed around him.
9.4: He fell to the ground and heard a voice say to him, “Saul (asked for), Saul, why do you persecute Me?
9.5: “Who are You, Lord {Kyrie}? Saul asked. “I am Jesus (YHWH saves), whom you are persecuting, He replied [“It is hard for you to kick against the goads.”].
9.6: “Now get up and go into the city, and you will be told what you must do.
9.7: The men traveling with Saul stood there speechless. They heard the voice but did not see anyone.
9.8: Saul (asked for) got up from the ground, but when he opened his eyes he could not see a thing \he could see nothing/. So they led him by the hand into Damascus (well-watered land).
9.9: For three days he was without sight, and he did not eat or drink anything.
9.10: In Damascus (well-watered land) there was a disciple (pupil) named Ananias (YHWH is gracious). The Lord {Kyrios} spoke to him in a vision, “Ananias! “Here I am, Lord {Kyrie}, he answered.
9.11: “Get up! the Lord {Kyrios} told him. “Go to the house of Judas (praise) on Straight Street and ask for a man from Tarsus named Saul (asked for), for he is praying.
9.12: In a vision he has seen a man named Ananias (YHWH is gracious) come and place his hands on him to restore his sight.
9.13: But Ananias (YHWH is gracious) answered, “Lord {Kyrie}, many people have told me about this man and all the harm he has done to Your saints (holy ones) in Jerusalem (city of peace).
9.14: And now he is here with authority from the chief priests to arrest all who call on Your name.
9.15: “Go! said the Lord {Kyrios}. “This man is My chosen instrument to carry My name before the Gentiles {ethn?n} and their kings, and before the people of Israel (He wrestles with God).
9.16: I will show him how much he must suffer for My name.
9.17: So Ananias (YHWH is gracious) went to the house, and when he arrived, he placed his hands on Saul. “Brother Saul (asked for), he said, “the Lord {Kyrios} Jesus (YHWH saves), who appeared to you on the road as you were coming here, has sent me so that you may see again and be filled with the Holy Spirit {Hagiou Pneumatos}.
9.18: At that instant, something like scales fell from Saul’s eyes, and his sight was restored. He got up and was baptized (submerged),
9.19: and after taking some food, he regained his strength. And he spent several days with the disciples (pupils) in Damascus (well-watered land).
9.20: Saul promptly began to proclaim Jesus (YHWH saves) in the synagogues, declaring, “He is the Son of God {Theou}.
9.21: All who heard him were astounded and asked, “Isn’t this the man who wreaked havoc in Jerusalem (city of peace) on those who call on this name? And hasn’t he come here to take them as prisoners to the chief priests?
9.22: But Saul (asked for) was empowered all the more, and he confounded the Jews (people of praise) living in Damascus (well-watered land) by proving that Jesus is the Christ (the Anointed One).
9.23: After many days had passed, the Jews (people of praise) conspired to kill him,
9.24: but Saul (asked for) learned of their plot. Day and night they watched the city gates in order to kill him.
9.25: One night, however, his disciples (pupils) took him and lowered him in a basket through a window in the wall \through the wall/.
9.26: When Saul arrived in Jerusalem (city of peace), he tried to join the disciples (pupils), but they were all afraid of him, not believing that he was a disciple (pupil).
9.27: Then Barnabas (son of encouragement) brought him to the apostles (sent ones) and described how Saul had seen the Lord {Kyrion}, who had spoken to him on the road to Damascus, and how Saul had spoken boldly in that city in the name of Jesus (YHWH saves).
9.28: So Saul stayed with them, moving about freely in Jerusalem (city of peace) and speaking boldly in the name of the Lord {Kyriou}.
9.29: He talked and debated with the Grecian Jews (Hellenists), but they tried to kill him.
9.30: When the brothers learned of this, they took him down to Caesarea and sent him off to Tarsus.
9.31: Then the church {ekkl?sia} throughout Judea (land of praise), Galilee (circuit), and Samaria experienced a time of peace. It grew in strength and numbers, living in the fear of the Lord {Kyriou} and the encouragement of the Holy Spirit {Hagiou Pneumatos}.
9.32: As Peter (rock) traveled throughout the area, he went to visit the saints (holy ones) in Lydda.
9.33: There he found a man named Aeneas who had been paralyzed and bedridden for eight years.
9.34: “Aeneas, Peter (rock) said to him, “Jesus (YHWH saves) Christ (the Anointed One) heals you! Get up and put away your mat. Immediately Aeneas got up,
9.35: and all who lived in Lydda and Sharon saw him and turned to the Lord {Kyrion}.
9.36: In Joppa (beautiful) there was a disciple (pupil) named Tabitha (gazelle) (which is translated as Dorcas), who was always occupied with works (acts) of kindness and charity.
9.37: At that time, however, she became sick and died, and her body was washed and placed in an upper room.
9.38: Since Lydda was near Joppa (beautiful), the disciples (pupils), hearing that Peter (rock) was there, sent two men to urge him, “Come to us without delay.
9.39: So Peter (rock) got up and went with them. On his arrival, they took him to the upper room. All the widows stood around him, weeping and showing him the tunics and other clothing that Dorcas (gazelle) had made while she was still with them.
9.40: Then Peter (rock) sent them all out of the room. He knelt down and prayed, and turning toward her body, he said, “Tabitha (gazelle), get up! She opened her eyes, and seeing Peter, she sat up.
9.41: Peter took her by the hand and helped her up. Then he called the saints (holy ones) and widows and presented her to them alive.
9.42: This became known all over Joppa (beautiful), and many people believed (had faith) in the Lord {Kyrion}.
9.43: And Peter stayed for several days in Joppa (beautiful) with a tanner named Simon (hearing).

10.1: At Caesarea there was a man named Cornelius (horn), a centurion (commander of a hundred) in what was called the Italian Regiment.
10.2: He and all his household were devout and God-fearing. He gave generously to the people and prayed to God {Theou} regularly.
10.3: One day at about the ninth hour (about 3 pm), he had a clear vision of an angel (messenger) of God {Theou} who came to him and said, “Cornelius (horn)!
10.4: Cornelius stared at him in fear and asked, “What is it, Lord {Kyrie}? The angel answered, “Your prayers and gifts to the poor have ascended as a memorial offering before God {Theou}.
10.5: Now send men to Joppa (beautiful) to call for a man named Simon (hearing) who is called Peter (rock).
10.6: He is staying with Simon (hearing) the tanner, whose house is by the sea [He will tell you what you need to do.].
10.7: When the angel (messenger) who spoke to him had gone, Cornelius called two of his servants and a devout soldier from among his attendants.
10.8: He explained what had happened and sent them to Joppa (beautiful).
10.9: The next day at about the sixth hour (about noon), as the men were approaching the city on their journey, Peter (rock) went up on the roof to pray.
10.10: He became hungry and wanted something to eat, but while the meal was being prepared, he fell into a trance.
10.11: He saw heaven open and something like a large sheet being let down to earth by its four corners.
10.12: It contained all kinds of four-footed animals and reptiles of the earth, as well as birds of the air.
10.13: Then a voice said to him: “Get up, Peter (rock), kill and eat!
10.14: “No, Lord {Kyrie}! Peter (rock) answered. “I have never eaten anything impure \common/ or unclean (impure).
10.15: The voice spoke to him a second time: “Do not call anything impure that God {Theos} has made clean.
10.16: This happened three times, and all at once the sheet was taken back up into heaven.
10.17: While Peter (rock) was puzzling over the meaning of the vision, the men sent by Cornelius (horn) found Simon’s house and approached the gate.
10.18: They called out to ask if Simon (hearing) called Peter (rock) was staying there.
10.19: As Peter (rock) continued to reflect on the vision, the Spirit {Pneuma} said to him, “Behold, three men are looking for you [two men are looking for you].
10.20: So get up! Go downstairs and accompany them without hesitation, because I have sent them.
10.21: So Peter (rock) went down to the men [the men sent to him by Cornelius] and said, “Here am I, the one you? are looking for. Why have you? come?
10.22: “Cornelius (horn) the centurion has sent us, they said. “He is a righteous and God-fearing {Theon} man with a good reputation among the whole Jewish nation {ethnous}. A holy angel (messenger) instructed him to request your presence in his home so he could hear a message {rh?mata} from you.
10.23: So Peter invited them in as his guests. And the next day he got ready and went with them, accompanied by some of the brothers from Joppa (beautiful).
10.24: The following day he arrived in Caesarea, where Cornelius (horn) was expecting them and had called together his relatives and close friends.
10.25: As Peter (rock) was about to enter, Cornelius (horn) met him and fell at his feet to worship him.
10.26: But Peter (rock) helped him up. “Stand up, he said, “I am only a man myself.
10.27: As Peter talked with him, he went inside and found many people gathered together.
10.28: He said to them, “You? know how unlawful it is for a Jew to associate with a foreigner or visit him. But God {Theos} has shown me that I should not call any man impure or unclean (impure).
10.29: So when I was invited, I came without objection. I ask, then, why have you? sent for me?
10.30: Cornelius (horn) answered: “Four days ago I was in my house praying at this, the ninth hour. Suddenly (Behold) a man in radiant clothing stood before me
10.31: and said, ‘Cornelius (horn), your prayer has been heard, and your gifts to the poor have been remembered before God {Theou}.
10.32: Therefore send to Joppa (beautiful) for Simon (hearing), who is called Peter (rock). He is a guest in the home of Simon the tanner, by the sea.
10.33: So I sent for you immediately, and you were kind enough to come. Now then, we are all here in the presence of God {Theou} to listen to everything the Lord {Kyriou} has instructed you to tell us.
10.34: Then Peter (rock) began to speak: “I now truly understand that God {Theos} does not show favoritism,
10.35: but welcomes those from every nation {ethnei} who fear Him and do what is right.
10.36: He has sent this message to the people of Israel (He wrestles with God), proclaiming the gospel (good news) of peace through Jesus (YHWH saves) Christ (the Anointed One), who is Lord {Kyrios} of all.
10.37: You? yourselves know what has happened {rh?ma} throughout Judea (land of praise), beginning in Galilee (circuit) with the baptism (submersion) that John (YHWH is gracious) proclaimed:
10.38: how God {Theos} anointed Jesus (YHWH saves) of Nazareth (branch) with the Holy Spirit {Hagi? Pneumati} and with power {dynamei}, and how Jesus went around doing good and healing all who were oppressed by the devil {diabolou}, because God was with Him.
10.39: We are witnesses of all that He did, both in the land of the Jews (people of praise) and in Jerusalem (city of peace). And although they put Him to death by hanging Him on a tree,
10.40: God {Theos} raised Him up on the third day and caused Him to be seen—
10.41: not by all the people, but by the witnesses God {Theou} had chosen beforehand, by us who ate and drank with Him after He rose from the dead.
10.42: And He commanded us to preach to the people and to testify that He is the One appointed by God {Theou} to judge the living and the dead.
10.43: All the prophets testify about Him that everyone who believes in Him receives forgiveness of sins through His name.
10.44: While Peter (rock) was still speaking these words {rh?mata}, the Holy Spirit {Hagion Pneuma} fell upon all who heard his message.
10.45: All the circumcised believers who had accompanied Peter (rock) were astounded that the gift of the Holy Spirit {Hagiou Pneumatos} had been poured out even on the Gentiles {ethn?}.
10.46: For they heard them speaking in tongues (languages) and exalting God {Theon}. Then Peter (rock) said,
10.47: “Can anyone withhold the water to baptize (submerge) these people? They have received the Holy Spirit {Hagion Pneuma} just as we have!
10.48: So he ordered that they be baptized (submerged) in the name of Jesus (YHWH saves) Christ (the Anointed One). Then they asked him to stay for a few days.

11.1: The apostles (sent ones) and brothers throughout Judea (land of praise) soon heard that the Gentiles {ethn?} also had received the word of God {Theou}.
11.2: So when Peter (rock) went up to Jerusalem (city of peace), the circumcised believers \those of the circumcision/ took issue with him
11.3: and said, “You visited uncircumcised men and ate with them.
11.4: But Peter (rock) began and explained to them the whole sequence of events:
11.5: “I was in the city of Joppa (beautiful) praying, and in a trance I saw a vision of something like a large sheet being let down from heaven by its four corners, and it came right down to me.
11.6: I looked at it closely and saw four-footed animals of the earth, wild beasts, reptiles, and birds of the air.
11.7: Then I heard a voice saying to me, ‘Get up, Peter (rock), kill and eat.
11.8: ‘No, Lord {Kyrie}, I said, ‘for nothing impure \common/ or unclean (impure) has ever entered my mouth.
11.9: But the voice spoke from heaven a second time, ‘Do not call anything impure that God {Theos} has made clean.
11.10: This happened three times, and everything was drawn back up into heaven.
11.11: Just then three men sent to me from Caesarea stopped at the house where I was staying.
11.12: The Spirit {Pneuma} told me to accompany them without hesitation. These six brothers also went with me, and we entered the man’s home.
11.13: He told us how he had seen an angel (messenger) standing in his house and saying, ‘Send to Joppa (beautiful) for Simon (hearing) who is called Peter (rock).
11.14: He will convey to you a message {rh?mata} by which you and all your household will be saved.
11.15: As I began to speak, the Holy Spirit {Hagion Pneuma} fell upon them, just as He had fallen upon us at the beginning.
11.16: Then I remembered the word {rh?matos} of the Lord {Kyriou}, how He said, ‘John (YHWH is gracious) baptized (submerged) with water, but you? will be baptized with the Holy Spirit {Hagi? Pneumati}.’
11.17: So if God {Theos} gave them the same gift He gave us who believed in the Lord {Kyrion} Jesus (YHWH saves) Christ (the Anointed One), who was I to hinder the work of God {Theon}?
11.18: When they heard this, they had no further objections, and they glorified God {Theon}, saying, “So then, God {Theos} has granted even the Gentiles {ethnesin} repentance unto life.
11.19: Meanwhile those scattered by the persecution that began with Stephen (that which surrounds) traveled as far as Phoenicia, Cyprus, and Antioch, speaking the message only to Jews (people of praise).
11.20: But some of them, men from Cyprus and Cyrene, went to Antioch and began speaking to the Greeks (the Hellenists) as well, proclaiming the good news (gospel) about the Lord {Kyrion} Jesus (YHWH saves).
11.21: The hand of the Lord {Kyriou} was with them, and a great number of people believed and turned to the Lord {Kyrion}.
11.22: When news of this reached the ears of the church {ekkl?sias} in Jerusalem (city of peace), they sent Barnabas (son of encouragement) to Antioch.
11.23: When he arrived and saw the grace of God {Theou}, he rejoiced and encouraged them all to abide in the Lord {Kyri?} with all their hearts.
11.24: Barnabas was a good man, full of the Holy Spirit {Hagiou Pneumatos} and faith, and a great number of people were brought to the Lord {Kyri?}.
11.25: Then Barnabas went to Tarsus to look for Saul (asked for),
11.26: and when he found him, he brought him back to Antioch. So for a full year they met together with the church {ekkl?sia} and taught {didaxai} large numbers of people. The disciples (pupils) were first called Christians at Antioch.
11.27: In those days some prophets came down from Jerusalem (city of peace) to Antioch.
11.28: One of them named Agabus stood up and predicted through the Spirit that a great famine would sweep across the whole world (the entire Roman world). (This happened under Claudius.)
11.29: So the disciples (pupils), each according to his ability, decided to send relief {diakonian} to the brothers (and sisters) living in Judea (land of praise).
11.30: This they did, sending their gifts to the elders with Barnabas (son of encouragement) and Saul (asked for).

12.1: About that time, King Herod (King Herod Agrippa) reached out to harm \put forth the hands to mistreat/ some who belonged to the church {ekkl?sias}.
12.2: He had James (supplanter / follower), the brother of John (YHWH is gracious), put to death with the sword.
12.3: And seeing that this pleased the Jews (people of praise), Herod proceeded to seize Peter (rock) during the Feast of Unleavened Bread.
12.4: He arrested him and put him in prison, handing him over to be guarded by four squads of four soldiers each. Herod intended to bring him out to the people after the Passover.
12.5: So Peter (rock) was kept in prison, but the church {ekkl?sias} was fervently praying to God {Theon} for him.
12.6: On the night before Herod (heroic) was to bring him to trial, Peter (rock) was sleeping between two soldiers, bound with two chains, with sentries standing guard at the entrance to the prison.
12.7: Suddenly an angel (messenger) of the Lord {Kyriou} appeared and a light shone in the cell. He tapped Peter (rock) on the side and woke him up, saying, “Get up quickly. And the chains fell off his wrists.
12.8: “Get dressed and put on your sandals, said the angel (messenger). Peter did so, and the angel told him, “Wrap your cloak around you and follow (accompany) me.
12.9: So Peter followed him out, but he was unaware that what the angel (messenger) was doing was real. He thought he was only seeing a vision.
12.10: They passed the first and second guards and came to the iron gate leading to the city, which opened for them by itself. When they had gone outside and walked the length of one block, the angel (messenger) suddenly left him.
12.11: Then Peter (rock) came to himself and said, “Now I know for sure that the Lord {Kyrios} has sent His angel (messenger) and rescued me from Herod’s grasp and from everything the Jewish people were anticipating.
12.12: And when he had realized this, he went to the house of Mary (beloved) the mother of John (YHWH is gracious), also called Mark, where many people had gathered together and were praying.
12.13: He knocked at the outer gate, and a servant girl named Rhoda (rose) came to answer it.
12.14: When she recognized Peter’s voice, she was so overjoyed that she forgot to open the gate, but ran inside and announced, “Peter (rock) is standing at the gate!
12.15: “You are out of your mind, they told her. But when she kept insisting it was so, they said, “It must be his angel (messenger).
12.16: But Peter (rock) kept on knocking, and when they opened the door and saw him, they were astounded.
12.17: Peter motioned with his hand for silence, and he described how the Lord {Kyrios} had brought him out of the prison. “Send word to James (supplanter / follower) and to the brothers, he said, and he left for another place.
12.18: At daybreak there was no small commotion among the soldiers as to what had become of Peter (rock).
12.19: After Herod (heroic) had searched for him unsuccessfully, he examined the guards and ordered that they be executed. Then he went down from Judea (land of praise) to Caesarea and spent some time there.
12.20: Now Herod was in a furious dispute (had become furious) with the people of Tyre and Sidon, and they convened before him. Having secured the support of Blastus, the king’s chamberlain, they asked for peace, because their region depended on the king’s country for food.
12.21: On the appointed day, Herod (heroic) donned his royal robes, sat on his throne, and addressed the people.
12.22: And they began to shout, “This is the voice of a god {Theou}, not a man!
12.23: Immediately, because Herod did not give glory {doxan} to God {The?}, an angel (messenger) of the Lord {Kyriou} struck him down, and he was eaten by worms and died.
12.24: But the word of God {theou} continued to spread and multiply.
12.25: When Barnabas (son of encouragement) and Saul (asked for) had fulfilled their mission {diakonian} to Jerusalem (city of peace), they returned, bringing with them John (YHWH is gracious), also called Mark.

13.1: Now in the church {ekkl?sian} at Antioch there were prophets and teachers {didaskaloi}: Barnabas (son of encouragement), Simeon called Niger, Lucius (light) of Cyrene, Manaen (who had been brought up with Herod (heroic) the tetrarch), and Saul (asked for).
13.2: While they were worshiping the Lord {Kyri?} and fasting, the Holy Spirit {Hagion Pneuma} said, “Set apart for Me Barnabas (son of encouragement) and Saul (asked for) for the work to which I have called them.
13.3: And after they had fasted and prayed, they laid their hands on them and sent them off.
13.4: So Barnabas and Saul, sent forth by the Holy Spirit {Hagiou Pneumatos}, went down to Seleucia and sailed from there to Cyprus.
13.5: When they arrived at Salamis, they proclaimed the word of God {Theou} in the Jewish synagogues. And John (YHWH is gracious) was with them as their helper.
13.6: They traveled through the whole island as far as Paphos, where they found a Jewish sorcerer and false prophet named Bar-Jesus,
13.7: an attendant of the proconsul, Sergius (servant) Paulus. The proconsul, a man of intelligence, summoned Barnabas (son of encouragement) and Saul (asked for) because he wanted to hear the word of God {Theou}.
13.8: But Elymas the sorcerer (for that is what his name means) opposed them and tried to turn the proconsul from the faith.
13.9: Then Saul (asked for), who was also called Paul (humble), filled with the Holy Spirit {Hagiou Pneumatos}, looked directly at Elymas
13.10: and said, “O child of the devil {diabolou} and enemy of all righteousness, you are full of all kinds of deceit and trickery! Will you never stop perverting the straight ways of the Lord {Kyriou}?
13.11: Now look (Behold), the hand of the Lord {Kyriou} is against you, and for a time you will be blind and unable to see the light of the sun. Immediately mist and darkness came over him, and he groped about, seeking someone to lead him by the hand.
13.12: When the proconsul saw what had happened, he believed (had faith), for he was astonished at the teaching {didach?} about the Lord {Kyriou}.
13.13: After setting sail from Paphos, Paul (humble) and his companions came to Perga in Pamphylia, where John (YHWH is gracious) left them to return to Jerusalem (city of peace).
13.14: And from Perga, they traveled inland to Pisidian Antioch, where they entered the synagogue on the Sabbath and sat down.
13.15: After the reading from the Law and the Prophets, the synagogue leaders sent word to them: “Brothers, if you? have a word of encouragement for the people, please speak.
13.16: Paul (humble) stood up, motioned with his hand, and began to speak: “Men of Israel (He wrestles with God) and you Gentiles who fear God {Theon}, listen to me!
13.17: The God {Theos} of the people of Israel (He wrestles with God) chose our fathers {pateras}. He made them into a great people during their stay in Egypt (land of bondage), and with an uplifted arm He led them out of that land.
13.18: He endured their conduct for about forty years in the wilderness (solitary place).
13.19: And having vanquished seven nations {ethn?} in Canaan (low), He gave their land to His people as an inheritance.
13.20: All this took about 450 years. After this, God gave them judges until the time of Samuel (God has heard) the prophet.
13.21: Then the people asked for a king, and God {Theos} gave them Saul (asked for) son of Kish, from the tribe of Benjamin (son of the right hand), who ruled forty years.
13.22: After removing Saul, He raised up David (beloved) as their king and testified about him: ‘I have found David son of Jesse (gift) a man after My own heart; he will carry out My will in its entirety <1Sa 13:14>.
13.23: From the descendants (seed) of this man, God {Theos} has brought to Israel (He wrestles with God) the Savior {S?t?ra} Jesus (YHWH saves), as He promised.
13.24: Before the arrival of Jesus, John (YHWH is gracious) preached a baptism (submersion) of repentance to all the people of Israel (He wrestles with God).
13.25: As John (YHWH is gracious) was completing his course, he said, ‘Who do you? suppose I am? I am not that One. But there is One coming after me whose sandals I am not worthy to untie .
13.26: Brothers, children of Abraham (father of many), and you? Gentiles who fear God {Theon}, it is to us that this message of salvation has been sent.
13.27: The people of Jerusalem (city of peace) and their rulers did not recognize Jesus, yet in condemning Him they fulfilled the words of the prophets that are read every Sabbath.
13.28: And though they found no ground for a death sentence, they asked Pilate (armed with a spear) to have Him executed.
13.29: When they had carried out all that was written about Him, they took Him down from the tree and laid Him in a tomb.
13.30: But God {Theos} raised Him from the dead,
13.31: and for many days He was seen by those who had accompanied Him from Galilee (circuit) to Jerusalem (city of peace). They are now His witnesses to our people.
13.32: And now we proclaim to you? the good news (gospel): What God promised our fathers {pateras}
13.33: He has fulfilled for us, their children, by raising up Jesus (YHWH saves). As it is written in the second Psalm: ‘You are My Son; today I have become Your Father .’
13.34: In fact, God raised Him from the dead, never to see decay. As He has said: ‘I will give you? the holy and sure blessings promised to David \I will give you the holy, the trustworthy of David/.’
13.35: So also, He says in another Psalm: ‘You will not let Your Holy One see decay .’
13.36: For when David (beloved) had served God’s {Theou} purpose in his own generation, he fell asleep. His body was buried with his fathers {pateras} and saw decay.
13.37: But the One whom God {Theos} raised from the dead did not see decay.
13.38: Therefore let it be known to you?, brothers, that through Jesus the forgiveness of sins is proclaimed to you?.
13.39: Through Him everyone who believes is justified from everything from which you? could not be justified (rendered innocent) by the law of Moses (drawn out).
13.40: Watch out, then, that what was spoken by the prophets does not happen to you:
13.41: ‘Look (behold), you scoffers, wonder and perish! For I am doing a work in your? days that you? would never believe, even if someone told you? .’
13.42: As Paul and Barnabas were leaving the synagogue, the people urged them to continue this message {rh?mata} on the next Sabbath.
13.43: After the synagogue was dismissed, many of the Jews (people of praise) and devout converts to Judaism followed Paul (humble) and Barnabas (son of encouragement), who spoke to them and urged them to continue in the grace of God {Theou}.
13.44: On the following Sabbath, nearly the whole city gathered to hear the word of the Lord {kyriou}.
13.45: But when the Jews (people of praise) saw the crowds, they were filled with jealousy, and they blasphemously contradicted what Paul (humble) was saying.
13.46: Then Paul (humble) and Barnabas (son of encouragement) answered them boldly: “It was necessary to speak the word of God {Theou} to you? first. But since you? reject it and do not consider yourselves worthy of eternal life, we now turn to the Gentiles {ethn?}.
13.47: For this is what the Lord {Kyrios} has commanded us: ‘I have made you a light for the Gentiles {ethn?n}, to bring salvation to the ends of the earth .’
13.48: When the Gentiles {ethn?} heard this, they rejoiced and glorified the word of the Lord {Kyriou}, and all who were appointed for eternal life believed (had faith).
13.49: And the word of the Lord {Kyriou} spread throughout that region.
13.50: The Jews (people of praise), however, incited the religious women of prominence and the leading men of the city. They stirred up persecution against Paul (humble) and Barnabas (son of encouragement) and drove them out of their district.
13.51: So they shook the dust off their feet in protest against them and went to Iconium.
13.52: And the disciples (pupils) were filled with joy and with the Holy Spirit {Hagiou Pneumatos}.

14.1: At Iconium, Paul and Barnabas went as usual into the Jewish synagogue, where they spoke so well that a great number of Jews (people of praise) and Greeks believed.
14.2: But the unbelieving Jews (people of praise) stirred up the Gentiles {ethn?n} and poisoned (afflicted) their minds {psychas} against the brothers.
14.3: So Paul and Barnabas spent considerable time there, speaking boldly for the Lord {Kyri?}, who affirmed the message of His grace by enabling them to perform signs and wonders.
14.4: The people of the city were divided. Some sided with the Jews (people of praise), and others with the apostles (sent ones).
14.5: But when the Gentiles {ethn?n} and Jews (people of praise), together with their rulers, set out to mistreat and stone them,
14.6: they found out about it and fled to the Lycaonian cities of Lystra and Derbe and to the surrounding region,
14.7: where they continued to preach the gospel (good news).
14.8: In Lystra there sat a man crippled in his feet, who was lame from birth and had never walked.
14.9: This man was listening to the words of Paul (humble), who looked intently at him and saw that he had faith to be healed.
14.10: In a loud voice Paul called out, “Stand up on your feet! And the man jumped up and began to walk.
14.11: When the crowds saw what Paul (humble) had done, they lifted up their voices in the Lycaonian language: “The gods {theoi} have come down to us in human form!
14.12: Barnabas (son of encouragement) they called Zeus, and Paul (humble) they called Hermes, because he was the chief speaker.
14.13: The priest of Zeus, whose temple was just outside the city, brought bulls and wreaths to the city gates, hoping to offer a sacrifice along with the crowds.
14.14: But when the apostles (sent ones) Barnabas (son of encouragement) and Paul (humble) found out about this, they tore their clothes and rushed into the crowd, shouting,
14.15: “Men, why are you? doing this? We too are only men, human like you?. We are bringing you? good news (gospel) that you? should turn from these worthless things to the living God {theon}, who made heaven and earth and sea and everything in them.
14.16: In past generations, He let all nations {ethn?} go their own way.
14.17: Yet He has not left Himself without testimony to His goodness: He gives you? rain from heaven and fruitful seasons, filling your? hearts with food and gladness.
14.18: Even with these words, Paul and Barnabas could hardly stop the crowds from sacrificing to them.
14.19: Then some Jews (people of praise) arrived from Antioch and Iconium and won over the crowds. They stoned Paul (humble) and dragged him outside the city, presuming he was dead.
14.20: But after the disciples (pupils) had gathered around him, he got up and went back into the city. And the next day he left with Barnabas (son of encouragement) for Derbe.
14.21: They preached the gospel to that city and made many disciples (pupils). Then they returned to Lystra, Iconium, and Antioch,
14.22: strengthening the souls {psychas} of the disciples (pupils) and encouraging them to continue in the faith. “We must endure many hardships to enter the kingdom of God {Theou}, they said.
14.23: Paul and Barnabas appointed elders for them in each church {ekkl?sian}, praying and fasting as they entrusted them to the Lord {Kyri?}, in whom they had believed.
14.24: After passing through Pisidia, they came to Pamphylia.
14.25: And when they had spoken the word in Perga, they went down to Attalia.
14.26: From Attalia they sailed to Antioch, where they had been commended to the grace of God {Theou} for the work they had just completed.
14.27: When they arrived, they gathered the church together {ekkl?sian} and reported all that God {Theos} had done through them, and how He had opened the door of faith to the Gentiles {ethnesin}.
14.28: And they spent a long time there with the disciples (pupils).

15.1: Then some men came down from Judea (land of praise) and were teaching the brothers, “Unless you? are circumcised according to the custom of Moses (drawn out), you? cannot be saved.
15.2: And after engaging these men in sharp debate, Paul (humble) and Barnabas (son of encouragement) were appointed, along with some other believers, to go up to Jerusalem (city of peace) to see the apostles (sent ones) and elders about this question.
15.3: Sent on their way by the church {ekkl?sias}, they passed through Phoenicia and Samaria, recounting the conversion of the Gentiles {ethn?n} and bringing great joy to all the brothers (and sisters).
15.4: On their arrival in Jerusalem (city of peace), they were welcomed by the church {ekkl?sias} and apostles (sent ones) and elders, to whom they reported all that God {Theos} had done through them.
15.5: But some believers from the party of the Pharisees stood up and declared, “The Gentiles must be circumcised and required to obey the law of Moses (drawn out).
15.6: So the apostles (sent ones) and elders met to look into this matter.
15.7: After much discussion, Peter (rock) got up and said to them, “Brothers, you? know that in the early days God {Theos} made a choice among you? that the Gentiles {ethn?} would hear from my lips the message of the gospel (good news) and believe.
15.8: And God {Theos}, who knows the heart, showed His approval by giving the Holy Spirit {Hagion Pneuma} to them, just as He did to us.
15.9: He made no distinction between us and them, for He cleansed their hearts by faith.
15.10: Now then, why do you? test God {Theon} by placing on the necks of the disciples (pupils) a yoke that neither we nor our fathers {pateres} have been able to bear?
15.11: On the contrary, we believe it is through the grace of the Lord {Kyriou} Jesus (YHWH saves) that we are saved, just as they are.
15.12: The whole assembly fell silent as they listened to Barnabas (son of encouragement) and Paul (humble) describing the signs and wonders God {Theos} had done among the Gentiles {ethnesin} through them.
15.13: When they had finished speaking, James (supplanter / follower) declared, “Brothers, listen to me!
15.14: Simon \Simeon/ has told us how God {Theos} first visited the Gentiles to take from them {ethn?n} a people to be His own.
15.15: The words of the prophets agree with this, as it is written:
15.16: ‘After this I will return and rebuild the fallen tent of David (beloved). Its ruins I will rebuild, and I will restore it,
15.17: so that the remnant of men may seek the Lord {Kyrion}, and all the Gentiles {ethn?} who are called by My name, says the Lord {Kyrios} who does these things
15.18: that have been known for ages.’
15.19: It is my judgment, therefore, that we should not cause trouble for the Gentiles {ethn?n} who are turning to God {Theon}.
15.20: Instead, we should write and tell them to abstain from food polluted by idols, from sexual immorality {porneias}, from the meat of strangled animals, and from blood.
15.21: For Moses (drawn out) has been proclaimed in every city from ancient times and is read in the synagogues on every Sabbath.
15.22: Then the apostles (sent ones) and elders, with the whole church {ekkl?sia}, decided to select men from among them to send to Antioch with Paul (humble) and Barnabas (son of encouragement). They chose Judas (praise) called Barsabbas and Silas, two leaders among the brothers,
15.23: and sent them with this letter: The apostles (sent ones) and the elders, your brothers, To the brothers among the Gentiles {ethn?n} in Antioch, Syria, and Cilicia: Greetings.
15.24: It has come to our attention that some went out from us without our authorization and unsettled you?, troubling your? minds {psychas} by what they said.
